untranslatable_reason: This signal describes an options spread strategy (diagonal bear call spread) with specific strike prices, expirations, and credit/debit mechanics. The backtest engine appears designed for price-based technical indicators on single instruments, not multi-leg derivative positions. Key untranslatable elements: (1) Near-term vs long-term call strikes and their relationship to underlying price; (2) Net credit received on entry; (3) Time decay and expiration-based exits; (4) Strike-relative profit/loss scenarios. Options spreads require pricing models and Greeks (delta, theta, vega) not represented in the allowed indicators.
translation_notes: This is an options strategy signal, not a price-action signal. It requires: options chain data, strike selection logic, greeks calculations, and expiration-based position management. A backtest engine designed for candlestick technicals cannot properly model diagonal spreads. Recommend: (1) Use a dedicated options backtesting library, or (2) Convert to directional proxy (e.g., 'short the underlying when technical conditions match the profitable scenario') if approximation is acceptable.
